title: Thelypteris linnaeana
taxonomy: Thelypteris linnaeana is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This species belongs to the order Blechnales. It falls under the class Polypodiopsida. The class is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. Thelypteris linnaeana is also known as the narrow-leaved wavy fern.
uses: Thelypteris linnaeana, also known as the Black Saddle Fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The leaves of the plant are also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, the plant is thought to have antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful in treating wounds and infections. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Thelypteris linnaeana for medicinal purposes.
